matches.sentence: Washington , February 15, 1955. Dear Cabot : Thank you for your letter of January 20, reporting your talk with the Afghan representative to the United Nations.

I do not, however, share the views of the Afghan government that the way to strengthen Afghanistan is to side with it against our ally Pakistan.
Instead, we believe that the only sound basis for improving the situation is to foster cooperation between Afghanistan and its southern neighbor.

However, the urgency of the problem requires that we consider providing assistance to cooperative economic projects which would draw the two countries together, strengthen Afghanistan’s ability to resist Soviet enticements, and in this way bring Afghanistan closer to the West.

However, the urgency of the problem requires that we consider providing assistance to cooperative economic projects which would draw the two countries together, strengthen Afghanistan’s ability to resist Soviet enticements, and in this way bring Afghanistan closer to the West.
To this end we are considering a $30 million program for which we might be able to draw on the proposed foreign aid contingency fund.

While we do not wish at this time to incur the risk of a strong Soviet reaction in Afghanistan nor to back the Afghans against the Pakistanis, we are most interested in drawing Afghanistan closer to the free world and intend to seize every reasonable opportunity for real progress in that direction.
